build.title: Player
build.subtitle: Character building: races, classes, feats, backgrounds

build.edSkillsClassHint: list skill names — matches grant proficiency

tables.title: Random Tables
tables.subtitle: Roll a die and read the result — loot, trinkets, rewards and your own tables
tables.fEntries: Table rows — one per line, format "1-5: text" or "7: text"
tables.entriesPh:
1-5: Nothing of interest.
6-15: A handful of coins.
16-20: A magic item.

hazards.title: Hazards
hazards.subtitle: Traps, poisons and diseases
hazards.tierIntroDamage: The higher the tier, the more this trap's own damage scales (click the dice to roll), the DC rises and effects are added. The base tier (2) is exactly as described above.
hazards.tierIntroNoDamage: This trap deals no damage — raising the tier only intensifies its effect and raises the DC.

ctor.expertiseHint: Choose {{n}} {{skill}} in which the proficiency bonus is doubled (only among those you are proficient in).
ctor.expertiseNoProf: First mark proficient skills in the "Skills" step.
ctor.equipmentHint: Purchased weapons appear automatically in the sheet's attacks (finesse weapons use the better of Strength/Dexterity).

guide.heading: Character Creation Guide
guide.subtitle: A step-by-step path from concept to finished hero by the 2024 Player's Handbook rules
guide.intro: This is not a reference, but a route. Follow the steps top to bottom; for details on races, classes, feats and backgrounds, switch to the neighboring tabs of this section.

step1.title: Hero Concept
step1.lead: Idea first, numbers later. Decide who your character is and why they set out on the road.
step1.point1: Who they are: origin, trade, dream and fear. What drove them from home?
step1.point2: What role do you want in the party — frontline fighter, mage, sly rogue, healer?
step1.point3: Agree with the GM on the setting, tone and starting level.

step2.title: Choose a Species (Race)
step2.lead: Species defines the hero's physical nature and innate traits.
step2.point1: Grants species traits: darkvision, lineages, size, speed, languages.
step2.point2: Important: in the 2024 rules, ability score increases come NOT from species, but from background.
step2.point3: Compare species and their traits on the “Races” tab.

step3.title: Choose a Class
step3.lead: Class is the character's profession and combat role, their main set of abilities.
step3.point1: Determines hit die, weapon and armor proficiencies, two saving throws and skills.
step3.point2: Grants key features by level; a subclass is usually chosen at 3rd level.
step3.point3: The class's primary ability — that's where to put your best score.
step3.point4: Full descriptions of classes and subclasses are on the “Classes” tab.

step4.title: Choose a Background
step4.lead: In the 2024 rules, background is the character's foundation, not just flavor.
step4.point1: Grants ability score increases: +2 and +1 (or +1/+1/+1) to three of your choice.
step4.point2: Grants proficiency in two skills, a tool, and an origin feat.
step4.point3: Includes starting equipment or gold to buy it.
step4.point4: Pick the one that fits your concept: soldier, sage, criminal, sailor… — the “Backgrounds” tab.

step5.title: Ability Scores
step5.lead: Six abilities: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma.
step5.point1: Standard array: 15, 14, 13, 12, 10, 8 — assign them to the abilities.
step5.point2: Point buy: 27 points, each ability from 8 to 15 before increases.
step5.point3: Rolling: 4d6, drop the lowest die, six times (with the GM's consent).
step5.point4: Then add the background increases. At 1st level an ability cannot exceed 20.

step6.title: Derived Values
step6.lead: From species, class and abilities the character's combat numbers are calculated.
step6.point1: Hit points at 1st level: maximum of the class hit die + Constitution modifier.
step6.point2: Armor Class: from armor and Dexterity (or a special class defense). Proficiency bonus at 1st level = +2.
step6.point3: Saving throw proficiencies come from class; skill proficiencies from class and background.
step6.point4: Passive Perception = 10 + Wisdom modifier (+ proficiency, if proficient).

step7.title: Equipment
step7.lead: Take the starting kit of your class or background — or take gold and buy equipment.
step7.point1: Weapons and armor for your combat role; a shield for defense.
step7.point2: For spellcasters — a focus or spellbook; an explorer's pack for everyone.
step7.point3: See prices, weight and item descriptions in the “Equipment” section.

step8.title: Spells
step8.lead: If your class can cast — pick your starting magic.
step8.point1: Choose cantrips and 1st-level spells from your class's list.
step8.point2: The spellcasting ability sets the save DC = 8 + proficiency + modifier.
step8.point3: Spell attack bonus = proficiency + spellcasting ability modifier.
step8.point4: Spell lists and descriptions are in the “Spells” section.

step9.title: Personality and Roleplay
step9.lead: The numbers are ready — breathe life into the character.
step9.point1: Write down a personality trait, ideal, bond and flaw.
step9.point2: Come up with a name, appearance, manner of speech and main goal in the adventure.
step9.point3: Tie the hero to the world, the GM's hook, and the rest of the party.

step10.title: Final Check
step10.lead: Gather everything on the character sheet and check with the GM.
step10.point1: Double-check modifiers, proficiencies, AC, hit points and saving throws.
step10.point2: Make sure the background increases are counted and the ability maximum is not exceeded.
step10.point3: Settle any disputed details with the GM — and off you go!

hub.title: Create a Character
hub.subtitle: Choose a method. The constructor only creates a character; after that you edit it manually.
hub.guideDesc: A step-by-step guide to creating a character: from concept to sheet.
hub.manualDesc: A blank sheet — fill in the fields yourself, save and print.
hub.constructorDesc: Choose race, class, level — abilities, skills, HP and attacks are calculated automatically.

hp.selectClassPrompt: Select a class to calculate hit points.

isheet.acManualTitle: Manual value. Auto AC including armor: {{n}}
isheet.acResetTitle: Reset to auto AC from armor ({{n}})
isheet.notesPlaceholder: Adventure log, goals, leads, NPC names…
isheet.magicItemsPlaceholder: Names of artifacts, potions, scrolls, their properties…

inv.denySimpleWeapons: Your class isn't proficient with simple weapons — attacks with them don't add your proficiency bonus.
inv.denyMartialWeapons: Your class isn't proficient with martial weapons — attacks with them don't add your proficiency bonus or a mastery property.
inv.armorDeny: Armor proficiency. Anyone can put on a suit of armor or strap a shield to an arm. Only those who know how to wear armor, however, can use it effectively. Your class gives you proficiency with certain types of armor. If you wear armor you lack proficiency with, you have Disadvantage on any ability check, saving throw, or attack roll that uses Strength or Dexterity, and you can't cast spells.
inv.pickClassHint: Choose a class to receive starting equipment or gold.
inv.shopHint: Pick an equipment folder. Purchased weapons appear in the sheet's attacks right away.
inv.categoryNotProficient: Your class isn't proficient with this category
inv.focusReminderPost: to cast spells with material components. Open the «Equipment» folder and buy a suitable focus (staff, crystal, wand, holy symbol, druidic focus) or a component pouch.
inv.emptyInventory: Empty — add background/class items or buy equipment.
inv.grantedLockTitle: Granted by class/background — remove it by switching to «Take gold»
inv.masteryEmpty: Get a weapon from your class/background or buy one to assign it a mastery property.

skill.pickClassFirst: Choose a class to select skills.
